Articles.Title: Farage resigns as MP and vows to fight by-election amid finances row
Articles.Summary: Labour, the Conservatives, the Lib Dems and Restore Britain rule out standing in the by-election, in an effective boycott.

Articles.Title: Chris Mason: Farage attempts to seize back the agenda
Articles.Summary: His aim throughout the forthcoming campaign is to clearly frame this by-election as "the people versus the establishment".

Articles.Title: US launches strikes on Iran after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
Articles.Summary: The US Central Command says the strikes are intended to impose "heavy costs" on Iran.

Articles.Title: Harry loses High Court privacy case against Daily Mail publisher
Articles.Summary: Judge Mr Justice Nicklin says the claimants had failed to prove the allegations.

Articles.Title: Trump looms large as Nato grapples with challenge of rearming Europe
Articles.Summary: The US president's inflammatory words have punctuated the image of unity at this crucial gathering, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner writes.

Articles.Title: Marine Le Pen to run for French presidency and appeal conviction in top court
Articles.Summary: The hard-right National Rally leader has ended months of speculation, after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.

Articles.Title: Woman suspected of Monaco bomb attack found dead in Ukraine
Articles.Summary: Police had been hunting Anastasiia Berezovska after a Ukrainian millionaire was injured in the blast.

Articles.Title: Watch: Clacton's mixed views on Farage's resignation
Articles.Summary: Nigel Farage announced he is resigning as an MP, triggering a by-election he will stand in.

Articles.Title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail
Articles.Summary: The lawyer for Dr Hussam Abu Safiya tells the BBC his client was so badly beaten he could not recognise him during a visit last week.

Articles.Title: Murder inquiry after mother and children found dead
Articles.Summary: A mother and her two children are found dead in a property, police say.

Articles.Title: Fifa investigating alleged racist abuse of IShowSpeed
Articles.Summary: Fifa has opened an investigation following allegations of racist abuse involving American social media influencer IShowSpeed and a supporter during Argentina's 3-2 win over Cape Verde on Friday.

Articles.Title: England players to be told not to jump hoardings
Articles.Summary: England players will be advised not to jump over advertising hoardings after Jordan Henderson suffered a freak injury.

Articles.Title: Can Scotland learn from history-making Norway?
Articles.Summary: Norway, a nation of a similar size to Scotland, are through to the World Cup quarter-finals, so can lessons be learned from their neighbours from across the North Sea?

Articles.Title: Jude Bellingham station to keep name until Monday
Articles.Summary: The station was renamed to give England a boost before the match with Mexico - in which Jude Bellingham shone.

Articles.Title: Harry's bad news lands at wrong time, but prince sticks to script
Articles.Summary: The Duke of Sussex has returned to the UK, but his first day of engagements was overshadowed by his defeat in court.

Articles.Title: Alan Carr is keeping one unwanted item from his castle auction – see what was sold
Articles.Summary: A concrete cow was the only one of the hundreds of items from Ayton Castle that failed to find a buyer.

Articles.Title: Presidency bid while continuing court fight a gamble for Le Pen
Articles.Summary: Hard-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing her case to France's top civil court, but it could backfire.

Articles.Title: The man who invented a sport and played tennis with Willy Wonka
Articles.Summary: Brad Parks came up with the idea for a new sport while on a family picnic 50 years ago.

Articles.Title: 'I wear it on my middle finger': The rise of the defiant divorce ring
Articles.Summary: Around the world women are marking their split with new - or repurposed engagement - rings.

Articles.Title: Hungary's public news broadcasts halted in bid to scrap Orban-era propaganda
Articles.Summary: The country's main state TV channel displayed a message saying it was "sorry" for lying on Tuesday.

Articles.Title: One million more UK homeowners set  to face higher mortgages
Articles.Summary: Homeowners face paying £45 a month more on average when they move onto a new deal over the next two years.

Articles.Title: Public to be asked who should pay for social care as part of major review
Articles.Summary: Baroness Louise Casey, who is leading the review, has called the current system "impossible".

Articles.Title: Online gamblers betting more than £1,000 to face new checks
Articles.Summary: Punters who bet more than £1,000 online in a 24-hour window will have to undergo an assessment, the regulator says.

Articles.Title: Russia could compete at LA 2028 Olympics
Articles.Summary: Russian athletes could be allowed to compete for their country at the 2028 Olympic Games in Los Angeles after the International Olympic Committee provisionally lifted their suspension.

Articles.Title: Teacher 'feared she would die' after being stabbed in head by pupil, court hears
Articles.Summary: A jury heard the 16-year-old launched at her with a large kitchen knife and stabbed her in the head.

Articles.Title: MI5 knew agent was misogynist 'obsessed' with violence, watchdog finds
Articles.Summary: A secret inquiry by the security service's watchdog concluded there were 'serious failings in MI5's management of Agent X’.

Articles.Title: BBC News app
Articles.Summary: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

Articles.Title: Nigel Farage Resigns As MP
Articles.Summary: Nigel Farage resigns as MP but says he’ll fight the by-election.

Articles.Title: After 250 years, do US citizens still believe in the American Dream?
Articles.Summary: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

Articles.Title: Tears of relief? Messi adds another incredible chapter to World Cup career
Articles.Summary: Argentina great Lionel Messi sheds tears as they fight back to beat Egypt and reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

Articles.Title: Superb England inflict record T20 defeat on India
Articles.Summary: England inflict a record defeat on India in the third Twenty20 at Trent Bridge to give the home side a 2-0 lead with two matches in the series remaining.

Articles.Title: 'How?!' - Gauff battles into first Wimbledon semi-final
Articles.Summary: Coco Gauff surprised even herself by overcoming her recent grass-court struggles to reach a first Wimbledon semi-final against Karolina Muchova.

Articles.Title: The fury and heartbreak of Egypt's stunning World Cup collapse
Articles.Summary: BBC Sport unpicks a game that will go down in World Cup history for featuring a team who were two goals down so late on, but who went on to win without needing extra time.

Articles.Title: Shades of Keane and Gerrard in Bellingham display - Rooney
Articles.Summary: Wayne Rooney believes Jude Bellingham's performance against Mexico ranks alongside iconic Steven Gerrard and Roy Keane displays
